sentence: I stopped ______ because the doctor told me to.
explanation: <Stop> + gerund = stop an activity permanently. He stopped smoking = he quit.

sentence: I stopped ______ some water because I was thirsty.
explanation: <Stop> + infinitive = pause one activity to do another. I stopped to drink = I paused to get water.
tip: stop + to = pause to do something else

sentence: I remember ______ the door before I left.
explanation: <Remember> + gerund = remember something you did in the past.
tip: remember + -ing = memory of a past action

sentence: Please remember ______ the door when you leave.
explanation: <Remember> + infinitive = don't forget to do something in the future.
tip: remember + to = don't forget a future action

sentence: I tried ______ the window, but it was stuck.
explanation: <Try> + infinitive = attempt something difficult.

sentence: I tried ______ the window to let some air in.
explanation: <Try> + gerund = experiment with a method or solution.

sentence: She forgot ______ her mother on her birthday.
explanation: <Forget> + infinitive = fail to do something you should do.
tip: forget + to = fail to do something

sentence: I'll never forget ______ the Northern Lights.
explanation: <Forget> + gerund = not forget a past experience or memory.

sentence: I regret ______ you that the position has been filled.
explanation: <Regret> + infinitive = feel sorry about what you are about to say (formal).
tip: regret + to = sorry to say (formal announcement)

sentence: She regrets ______ school so young.
explanation: <Regret> + gerund = feel sorry about something you did in the past.
tip: regret + -ing = sorry about a past action

sentence: He went on ______ about his holiday for hours.
explanation: <Go on> + gerund = continue the same activity.
tip: go on + -ing = continue the same thing

sentence: After the introduction, she went on ______ the main results.
explanation: <Go on> + infinitive = move to the next activity or topic.
tip: go on + to = move to the next thing

sentence: Being a doctor means ______ long hours.
explanation: <Mean> + gerund = involve or result in something.

sentence: I didn't mean ______ your feelings.
explanation: <Mean> + infinitive = intend to do something.

sentence: The children stopped ______ when the teacher entered.
explanation: <Stop> + gerund = cease the activity. They stopped talking = they became quiet.

sentence: On the way home, we stopped ______ petrol.
explanation: <Stop> + infinitive = pause in order to do something. We stopped to get petrol = we pulled over.
tip: stop + to = pause for a purpose

sentence: Do you remember ______ this song at school?
explanation: <Remember> + gerund = have a memory of doing something.

sentence: You must remember ______ your passport.
explanation: <Remember> + infinitive = not forget a future obligation.
tip: remember + to = don't forget to do it

sentence: If the computer crashes, try ______ it off and on again.
explanation: <Try> + gerund = experiment with a possible solution.

sentence: I tried ______ her, but her phone was off.
explanation: <Try> + infinitive = make an effort to do something.

sentence: I'll never forget ______ my first car.
explanation: <Forget> + gerund = a memorable past experience you won't forget.

sentence: Don't forget ______ the lights before bed.
explanation: <Forget> + infinitive = fail to remember a task.
tip: forget + to = remember to do it

sentence: We regret ______ that the flight has been cancelled.
explanation: <Regret> + infinitive = formal way to deliver bad news.

sentence: He regrets not ______ harder at university.
explanation: <Regret> + gerund = feel sorry about a past action or inaction.
tip: regret + -ing = wish you had done differently

sentence: Winning the lottery would mean ______ to work.
explanation: <Mean> + gerund = involve as a consequence.
